verses.latin_vulgate: Erat autem homo ex pharisæis, Nicodemus nomine, princeps Judæorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν δὲ ἄνθρωπος ἐκ τῶν φαρισαίων νικόδημος ὄνομα αὐτῷ ἄρχων τῶν ἰουδαίων
verses.king_james: There was a man of the Pharisees, named Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews:

verses.latin_vulgate: Hic venit ad Jesum nocte, et dixit ei: Rabbi, scimus quia a Deo venisti magister, nemo enim potest hæc signa facere, quæ tu facis, nisi fuerit Deus cum eo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτος ἦλθεν πρὸς τὸν ἰησοῦν νυκτὸς καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ ῥαββί οἴδαμεν ὅτι ἀπὸ θεοῦ ἐλήλυθας διδάσκαλος οὐδεὶς γὰρ ταῦτα τὰ σημεῖα δύναται ποιεῖν ἃ σὺ ποιεῖς ἐὰν μὴ ᾖ ὁ θεὸς μετ᾽ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: The same came to Jesus by night, and said unto him, Rabbi, we know that thou art a teacher come from God: for no man can do these miracles that thou doest, except God be with him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Jesus, et dixit ei: Amen, amen dico tibi, nisi quis renatus fuerit denuo, non potest videre regnum Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ὁ ἰησοῦς καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ ἀμὴν ἀμὴν λέγω σοι ἐὰν μή τις γεννηθῇ ἄνωθεν οὐ δύναται ἰδεῖν τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: Jesus answered and said unto him, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ad eum Nicodemus: Quomodo potest homo nasci, cum sit senex? numquid potest in ventrem matris suæ iterato introire et renasci?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει πρὸς αὐτὸν ὁ νικόδημος πῶς δύναται ἄνθρωπος γεννηθῆναι γέρων ὤν μὴ δύναται εἰς τὴν κοιλίαν τῆς μητρὸς αὐτοῦ δεύτερον εἰσελθεῖν καὶ γεννηθῆναι
verses.king_james: Nicodemus saith unto him, How can a man be born when he is old? can he enter the second time into his mother's womb, and be born?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Jesus: Amen, amen dico tibi, nisi quis renatus fuerit ex aqua, et Spiritu Sancto, non potest introire in regnum Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ὁ ἰησοῦς ἀμὴν ἀμὴν λέγω σοι ἐὰν μή τις γεννηθῇ ἐξ ὕδατος καὶ πνεύματος οὐ δύναται εἰσελθεῖν εἰς τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: Jesus answered, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born of water and *of* the Spirit, he cannot enter into the kingdom of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod natum est ex carne, caro est: et quod natum est ex spiritu, spiritus est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸ γεγεννημένον ἐκ τῆς σαρκὸς σάρξ ἐστιν καὶ τὸ γεγεννημένον ἐκ τοῦ πνεύματος πνεῦμά ἐστιν
verses.king_james: That which is born of the flesh is flesh; and that which is born of the Spirit is spirit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non mireris quia dixi tibi: oportet vos nasci denuo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ θαυμάσῃς ὅτι εἶπόν σοι δεῖ ὑμᾶς γεννηθῆναι ἄνωθεν
verses.king_james: Marvel not that I said unto thee, Ye must be born again.

verses.latin_vulgate: Spiritus ubi vult spirat, et vocem ejus audis, sed nescis unde veniat, aut quo vadat: sic est omnis qui natus est ex spiritu.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸ πνεῦμα ὅπου θέλει πνεῖ καὶ τὴν φωνὴν αὐτοῦ ἀκούεις ἀλλ᾽ οὐκ οἶδας πόθεν ἔρχεται καὶ ποῦ ὑπάγει οὕτως ἐστὶν πᾶς ὁ γεγεννημένος ἐκ τοῦ πνεύματος
verses.king_james: The wind bloweth where it listeth, and thou hearest the sound thereof, but canst not tell whence it cometh, and whither it goeth: so is every one that is born of the Spirit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Nicodemus, et dixit ei: Quomodo possunt hæc fieri?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη νικόδημος καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ πῶς δύναται ταῦτα γενέσθαι
verses.king_james: Nicodemus answered and said unto him, How can these things be?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Jesus, et dixit ei: Tu es magister in Israël, et hæc ignoras?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ὁ ἰησοῦς καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ σὺ εἶ ὁ διδάσκαλος τοῦ ἰσραὴλ καὶ ταῦτα οὐ γινώσκεις
verses.king_james: Jesus answered and said unto him, Art thou a master of Israel, and knowest not these things?

verses.latin_vulgate: amen, amen dico tibi, quia quod scimus loquimur, et quod vidimus testamur, et testimonium nostrum non accipitis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀμὴν ἀμὴν λέγω σοι ὅτι ὃ οἴδαμεν λαλοῦμεν καὶ ὃ ἑωράκαμεν μαρτυροῦμεν καὶ τὴν μαρτυρίαν ἡμῶν οὐ λαμβάνετε
verses.king_james: Verily, verily, I say unto thee, We speak that we do know, and testify that we have seen; and ye receive not our witness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si terrena dixi vobis, et non creditis: quomodo, si dixero vobis cælestia, credetis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ τὰ ἐπίγεια εἶπον ὑμῖν καὶ οὐ πιστεύετε πῶς ἐὰν εἴπω ὑμῖν τὰ ἐπουράνια πιστεύσετε
verses.king_james: If I have told you earthly things, and ye believe not, how shall ye believe, if I tell you *of* heavenly things?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nemo ascendit in cælum, nisi qui descendit de cælo, Filius hominis, qui est in cælo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὐδεὶς ἀναβέβηκεν εἰς τὸν οὐρανὸν εἰ μὴ ὁ ἐκ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ καταβάς ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ὁ ὤν ἐν τῷ οὐρανῷ
verses.king_james: And no man hath ascended up to heaven, but he that came down from heaven, *even* the Son of man which is in heaven.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et sicut Moyses exaltavit serpentem in deserto, ita exaltari oportet Filium hominis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ καθὼς μωσῆς ὕψωσεν τὸν ὄφιν ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ οὕτως ὑψωθῆναι δεῖ τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ ἀνθρώπου
verses.king_james: And as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, even so must the Son of man be lifted up:

verses.latin_vulgate: ut omnis qui credit in ipsum, non pereat, sed habeat vitam æternam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα πᾶς ὁ πιστεύων εἰς αὐτὸν μὴ ἀπόληται, ἀλλ᾽ ἔχῃ ζωὴν αἰώνιον
verses.king_james: That whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have eternal life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sic enim Deus dilexit mundum, ut Filium suum unigenitum daret: ut omnis qui credit in eum, non pereat, sed habeat vitam æternam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὕτως γὰρ ἠγάπησεν ὁ θεὸς τὸν κόσμον ὥστε τὸν υἱὸν αὐτοῦ τὸν μονογενῆ ἔδωκεν ἵνα πᾶς ὁ πιστεύων εἰς αὐτὸν μὴ ἀπόληται ἀλλ᾽ ἔχῃ ζωὴν αἰώνιον
verses.king_james: For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim misit Deus Filium suum in mundum, ut judicet mundum, sed ut salvetur mundus per ipsum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ γὰρ ἀπέστειλεν ὁ θεὸς τὸν υἱὸν αὐτοῦ εἰς τὸν κόσμον ἵνα κρίνῃ τὸν κόσμον ἀλλ᾽ ἵνα σωθῇ ὁ κόσμος δι᾽ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: For God sent not his Son into the world to condemn the world; but that the world through him might be saved.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui credit in eum, non judicatur; qui autem non credit, jam judicatus est: quia non credit in nomine unigeniti Filii Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ πιστεύων εἰς αὐτὸν οὐ κρίνεται ὁ δὲ μὴ πιστεύων ἤδη κέκριται ὅτι μὴ πεπίστευκεν εἰς τὸ ὄνομα τοῦ μονογενοῦς υἱοῦ τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: He that believeth on him is not condemned: but he that believeth not is condemned already, because he hath not believed in the name of the only begotten Son of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hoc est autem judicium: quia lux venit in mundum, et dilexerunt homines magis tenebras quam lucem: erant enim eorum mala opera.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: αὕτη δέ ἐστιν ἡ κρίσις ὅτι τὸ φῶς ἐλήλυθεν εἰς τὸν κόσμον καὶ ἠγάπησαν οἱ ἄνθρωποι μᾶλλον τὸ σκότος ἢ τὸ φῶς ἦν γὰρ πονηρὰ αὐτῶν τὰ ἔργα
verses.king_james: And this is the condemnation, that light is come into the world, and men loved darkness rather than light, because their deeds were evil.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis enim qui male agit, odit lucem, et non venit ad lucem, ut non arguantur opera ejus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πᾶς γὰρ ὁ φαῦλα πράσσων μισεῖ τὸ φῶς καὶ οὐκ ἔρχεται πρὸς τὸ φῶς ἵνα μὴ ἐλεγχθῇ τὰ ἔργα αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: For every one that doeth evil hateth the light, neither cometh to the light, lest his deeds should be reproved.

verses.latin_vulgate: qui autem facit veritatem, venit ad lucem, ut manifestentur opera ejus, quia in Deo sunt facta.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ποιῶν τὴν ἀλήθειαν ἔρχεται πρὸς τὸ φῶς ἵνα φανερωθῇ αὐτοῦ τὰ ἔργα ὅτι ἐν θεῷ ἐστιν εἰργασμένα
verses.king_james: But he that doeth truth cometh to the light, that his deeds may be made manifest, that they are wrought in God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Post hæc venit Jesus et discipuli ejus in terram Judæam: et illic demorabatur cum eis, et baptizabat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ ταῦτα ἦλθεν ὁ ἰησοῦς καὶ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ εἰς τὴν ἰουδαίαν γῆν καὶ ἐκεῖ διέτριβεν μετ᾽ αὐτῶν καὶ ἐβάπτιζεν
verses.king_james: After these things came Jesus and his disciples into the land of Judaea; and there he tarried with them, and baptized.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erat autem et Joannes baptizans, in Ænnon, juxta Salim: quia aquæ multæ erant illic, et veniebant et baptizabantur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν δὲ καὶ ἰωάννης βαπτίζων ἐν αἰνὼν ἐγγὺς τοῦ σαλείμ ὅτι ὕδατα πολλὰ ἦν ἐκεῖ καὶ παρεγίνοντο καὶ ἐβαπτίζοντο
verses.king_james: And John also was baptizing in Aenon near to Salim, because there was much water there: and they came, and were baptized.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nondum enim missus fuerat Joannes in carcerem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὔπω γὰρ ἦν βεβλημένος εἰς τὴν φυλακὴν ὁ ἰωάννης
verses.king_james: For John was not yet cast into prison.

verses.latin_vulgate: Facta est autem quæstio ex discipulis Joannis cum Judæis de purificatione.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγένετο οὖν ζήτησις ἐκ τῶν μαθητῶν ἰωάννου μετὰ ἰουδαίων περὶ καθαρισμοῦ
verses.king_james: Then there arose a question between *some* of John's disciples and the Jews about purifying.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et venerunt ad Joannem, et dixerunt ei: Rabbi, qui erat tecum trans Jordanem, cui tu testimonium perhibuisti, ecce hic baptizat, et omnes veniunt ad eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἦλθον πρὸς τὸν ἰωάννην καὶ εἶπον αὐτῷ ῥαββί ὃς ἦν μετὰ σοῦ πέραν τοῦ ἰορδάνου ᾧ σὺ μεμαρτύρηκας ἴδε οὗτος βαπτίζει καὶ πάντες ἔρχονται πρὸς αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And they came unto John, and said unto him, Rabbi, he that was with thee beyond Jordan, to whom thou barest witness, behold, the same baptizeth, and all *men* come to him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Joannes, et dixit: Non potest homo accipere quidquam, nisi fuerit ei datum de cælo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ἰωάννης καὶ εἶπεν οὐ δύναται ἄνθρωπος λαμβάνειν οὐδὲν ἐὰν μὴ ᾖ δεδομένον αὐτῷ ἐκ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ
verses.king_james: John answered and said, A man can receive nothing, except it be given him from heaven.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipsi vos mihi testimonium perhibetis, quod dixerim: Non sum ego Christus: sed quia missus sum ante illum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: αὐτοὶ ὑμεῖς μοι μαρτυρεῖτε ὅτι εἶπον οὐκ εἰμὶ ἐγὼ ὁ χριστός ἀλλ᾽ ὅτι ἀπεσταλμένος εἰμὶ ἔμπροσθεν ἐκείνου
verses.king_james: Ye yourselves bear me witness, that I said, I am not the Christ, but that I am sent before him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui habet sponsam, sponsus est: amicus autem sponsi, qui stat, et audit eum, gaudio gaudet propter vocem sponsi. Hoc ergo gaudium meum impletum est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ ἔχων τὴν νύμφην νυμφίος ἐστίν ὁ δὲ φίλος τοῦ νυμφίου ὁ ἑστηκὼς καὶ ἀκούων αὐτοῦ χαρᾷ χαίρει διὰ τὴν φωνὴν τοῦ νυμφίου αὕτη οὖν ἡ χαρὰ ἡ ἐμὴ πεπλήρωται
verses.king_james: He that hath the bride is the bridegroom: but the friend of the bridegroom, which standeth and heareth him, rejoiceth greatly because of the bridegroom's voice: this my joy therefore is fulfilled.

verses.latin_vulgate: Illum oportet crescere, me autem minui.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐκεῖνον δεῖ αὐξάνειν ἐμὲ δὲ ἐλαττοῦσθαι
verses.king_james: He must increase, but I *must* decrease.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui desursum venit, super omnes est. Qui est de terra, de terra est, et de terra loquitur. Qui de cælo venit, super omnes est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ ἄνωθεν ἐρχόμενος ἐπάνω πάντων ἐστίν ὁ ὢν ἐκ τῆς γῆς ἐκ τῆς γῆς ἐστιν καὶ ἐκ τῆς γῆς λαλεῖ ὁ ἐκ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ ἐρχόμενος ἐπάνω πάντων ἐστίν
verses.king_james: He that cometh from above is above all: he that is of the earth is earthly, and speaketh of the earth: he that cometh from heaven is above all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et quod vidit, et audivit, hoc testatur: et testimonium ejus nemo accipit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὃ ἑώρακεν καὶ ἤκουσεν τοῦτο μαρτυρεῖ καὶ τὴν μαρτυρίαν αὐτοῦ οὐδεὶς λαμβάνει
verses.king_james: And what he hath seen and heard, that he testifieth; and no man receiveth his testimony.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui accepit ejus testimonium signavit, quia Deus verax est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ λαβὼν αὐτοῦ τὴν μαρτυρίαν ἐσφράγισεν ὅτι ὁ θεὸς ἀληθής ἐστιν
verses.king_james: He that hath received his testimony hath set to his seal that God is true.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quem enim misit Deus, verba Dei loquitur: non enim ad mensuram dat Deus spiritum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃν γὰρ ἀπέστειλεν ὁ θεὸς τὰ ῥήματα τοῦ θεοῦ λαλεῖ οὐ γὰρ ἐκ μέτρου δίδωσιν ὁ θεὸς, τὸ πνεῦμα
verses.king_james: For he whom God hath sent speaketh the words of God: for God giveth not the Spirit by measure *unto him*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pater diligit Filium et omnia dedit in manu ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ πατὴρ ἀγαπᾷ τὸν υἱόν καὶ πάντα δέδωκεν ἐν τῇ χειρὶ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: The Father loveth the Son, and hath given all things into his hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui credit in Filium, habet vitam æternam; qui autem incredulus est Filio, non videbit vitam, sed ira Dei manet super eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ πιστεύων εἰς τὸν υἱὸν ἔχει ζωὴν αἰώνιον ὁ δὲ ἀπειθῶν τῷ υἱῷ οὐκ ὄψεται ζωήν ἀλλ᾽ ἡ ὀργὴ τοῦ θεοῦ μένει ἐπ᾽ αὐτόν
verses.king_james: He that believeth on the Son hath everlasting life: and he that believeth not the Son shall not see life; but the wrath of God abideth on him.
